title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry scalp

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with dry scalp because of their unique properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The gel-like substance within aloe vera has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing benefits, which can help to reduce flaking and itchiness associated with a dry scalp. As the capsule is absorbed into the bloodstream, it delivers these beneficial compounds directly to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing redness and irritation. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief from the discomfort and embarrassment of a dry scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on the scalp, soothing itchiness and discomfort caused by dry scalp conditions.
Hydrating property: The natural moisturizing agents in herbal aloe capsules help lock in moisture, nourish, and hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness, flakiness, and dandruff.
Antimicrobial property: Herbal aloe capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that combat fungal, bacterial, and yeast infections on the scalp, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosides help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Saponins: These natural surfactants help to gently cleanse the scalp, removing dirt and oils that can clog pores and contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Polysaccharides (specifically, glucomannans): These complex sugars help to lock in moisture and soothe the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their gel content, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Gel from Leaves: Also used for its anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and promote healthy scalp growth.
Sap from Leaves: Used for its moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry scalp irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 to 200 grams of aloe vera gel from mature leaves.
Dry the harvested aloe vera gel in a low temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Weigh and mix 100 grams of dried aloe vera gel with 100 grams of vegetable capsules.
Fill each capsule shell with 250 milligrams of aloe vera gel mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with dry scalp because they nourish the hair follicles, promoting healthy growth and rejuvenation. The herbaceous properties in Brahmi help to regulate the scalp's natural moisture balance, reducing flakiness and itchiness caused by dryness. Additionally, Brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated scalps, while its antioxidant benefits protect against environmental stressors that can exacerbate dryness. By addressing these underlying causes, Brahmi capsules effectively alleviate dry scalp concerns, leaving hair looking healthy, shiny, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry scalp conditions such as dandruff and psoriasis.

Stress-Reducing and Calming Effects: Brahmi's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties can help to alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for dry scalp issues such as eczema and seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: These triterpene saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to dry scalp.

Bacopasides: These triterpene glycosides are known for their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy scalp function and preventing dryness.

Alkaloids (Bacopine): These alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and infection in the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bacosides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and may help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Roots: The roots contain bacosides and other compounds that may help to calm and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and irritation.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri are also a source of bacosides, which may help to regulate the scalp's natural moisture balance and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 250 grams of dried bacopa monnieri roots in a large bowl.
Blend the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 30 grams of gelatin powder in a separate bowl.
Add 10 grams of glycerin and 10 grams of distilled water to the gelatin mixture.
Fill 100 capsule shells with the gelatin mixture and seal the capsules within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with dry scalp because they are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and moisturize the scalp. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy and flaky scalp, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, their humectant properties attract and retain moisture, leaving the scalp feeling soft and supple. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, maidenhair tree capsules help to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and dandruff, leading to a smoother, healthier-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic effects, helping to soothe and calm an itchy and inflamed dry scalp.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have antimicrobial effects, fighting off fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpenoid constituent helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the scalp, which may alleviate dryness and irritation.
Ginkgolides: These diterpenoid lactones possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness associated with dry scalp.

Quercetin: This flavonoid phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to promote scalp health.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are known to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm an itchy, dry scalp.
Barks: Ginkgo barks are sometimes used in traditional medicine for their antioxidant properties, which may help protect the scalp from damage and promote overall scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 200 milligrams of vegetable cellulose and 10 milligrams of silicon dioxide.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. The capsules contain stinging nettle extract, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp conditions. By reducing inflammation, the capsules help to soothe the scalp and promote a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, stinging nettle's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to eliminate any underlying fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to the dry scalp condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sterols, help reduce inflammation and soothe itching and redness associated with a dry scalp.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of stinging nettle capsules help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, irritation, and flaking.
Vaso-dilating property: Stinging nettle's ability to dilate blood vessels may help increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth, reducing dandruff, and calming itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Urtica dioica help with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment, which can alleviate symptoms of dryness and itchiness.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, in Urtica dioica capsules help with dry scalp by acting as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress, and improving blood flow to the scalp, which can help to promote a healthy scalp.

Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica help with dry scalp by having anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to calm irritation and reduce flaking, promoting a healthier scalp and relieving dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, they help soothe and calm the scalp.
Roots: High in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that can help treat dry scalp conditions.
Stems: Contain a high amount of histamine, which can help stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems from a trusted source in the morning.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1/4 cup of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1/2 teaspoon of the fine powder with 1/2 teaspoon of cellulose in a small bowl.
Fill a gelatin capsule with the mixture and store it in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and irritation. The curcumin in turmeric capsules also has antifungal and antibacterial effects, helping to combat the underlying causes of dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties promote healthy skin cell turnover, reducing flakiness and itchiness, and leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation associated with a dry scalp.
Cineole: This natural compound has antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective in combating fungal infections that can cause itching and flaking on the scalp.
Volatile oils: The essential oils present in turmeric capsules have moisturizing and nourishing properties, which can help to hydrate and soften the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting healthy hair growth.

Tumerone: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dry scalp, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing the risk of dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are another commonly used part, rich in curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds that help to soothe and treat dry scalp conditions.
Buds: The buds are often used in traditional medicine to treat various skin conditions, including dry scalp, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried curcuma longa root powder from reputable sources.
Weigh 5 grams of the powder and mix it with 5 grams of starch powder in a bowl.
Combine the powder mixture with 5 grams of gelatin and 10 ml of glycerin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes while stirring constantly with a spatula.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool before filling gelatin capsules with the mixture.

Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole helps with dry scalp by its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which reduce redness and promote blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: Used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp skin.
Stems: Used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 60 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves for 1 to 2 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 to 15 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 100 to 200 milliliters of a binding agent such as gelatin or starch.
Fill small gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them using a capsule machine.

These properties help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions. Additionally, tea tree capsules may also help to balance the scalp's natural pH level, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal and bacterial growth, thereby promoting healthy hair growth and alleviating dry scalp symptoms.

remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help to control fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, thereby reducing irritation and dryness.
Linalool: This terpene exhibits anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are commonly used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and treat dry scalp.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain tea tree oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off scalp infections and promote healthy scalp growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers at the peak of potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a temperature-controlled environment at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 50g of the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 10g of the ground powder with 10g of a natural filler such as gum arabic and 5g of a natural binder such as starch.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 250mg of the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggulu capsules helps with dry scalp because they promote healthy scalp circulation, which in turn increases sebum production to moisturize the scalp. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and itching associated with dry scalp. Additionally, guggulu capsules help to unclog pores, allowing the scalp to breathe and reducing flakiness. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Guggulu's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing a dry scalp.

Antioxidant: Guggulu's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the scalp and hair follicles, leading to dryness and breakage. By reducing oxidative stress, Guggulu helps promote healthy scalp function.

Purifying and detoxifying: Guggulu's purifying and detoxifying properties help remove toxins and impurities from the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and irritation. By cleansing the scalp, Guggulu helps create a healthier environment for hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: Glycosides present in Commiphora wightii help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Terpenoids (specifically, alpha-amyrin and beta-amyrin): Terpenoids in Commiphora wightii capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, leading to a healthier scalp.

Furanosesquiterpenes (specifically, furanodiene and furanodienone): Furanosesquiterpenes in Commiphora wightii have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to control flaking and itching associated with a dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Commiphora wightii are commonly used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their high guggulsterone content, which is believed to promote scalp health.
Barks: The barks of Commiphora wightii are used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated scalp.
Seeds: The seeds of Commiphora wightii are used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their ability to promote hair growth and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100 grams of dried commiphora wightii root bark into 500 milliliters of water using a double boiler for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to obtain a herbal extract in 30 minutes.
Combine the extract with 1 gram of gum arabic in 100 milliliters of water to enhance capsule formation in 10 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract mixture in 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

Leaves: Used for making capsules to treat dry scalp due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce irritation and promote a healthy scalp.
Seeds: Used for making capsules to treat dry scalp due to its emollient properties, which help moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of trigonella foenum graecum leaves and flowers at peak potency for best results.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 2 ounces of the dried trigonella foenum graecum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of distilled water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal according to the manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When used in herbal form, it can soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dryness. The capsules also help to balance the natural pH of the scalp, creating an environment that encourages healthy hair growth and reduces the risk of fungal infections. remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with a dry scalp.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have moisturizing and nourishing effects, which can help hydrate the scalp and promote healthy skin growth, reducing flakiness and dryness.
Saponins: These compounds have emollient and humectant properties, which can help lock in moisture, soothe irritated skin, and protect the scalp from further drying and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting skin hydration, thereby soothing irritation and itchiness associated with dry scalp.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier to ensure quality and potency.
Dry the harvested roots for 2-3 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to increase bioavailability.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of a natural binder such as tapioca starch to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler and seal the capsules to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with dry scalp because of their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract, also known as Calendula officinalis, is deeply moisturizing and soothing, which helps to calm irritated skin and reduce flaking. As the capsules are absorbed into the bloodstream, they work to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting a healthy balance of natural oils and reducing dryness and itchiness. This natural remedy can help to restore a smooth, shiny hair texture and alleviate the discomfort associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce inflammation, which helps to soothe an itchy and flaky scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The active compounds in pot marigold capsules, such as luteolin and apigenin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial agents that can contribute to a dry and irritated scalp.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Pot marigold's fatty acid content helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness on the scalp while also promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.

Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective against dry scalp infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and sift through them to remove any debris.
Measure out 100g of the calendula flowers and combine them with 200ml of boiling water in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding solids.
Mix 15ml of the strained liquid with 45g of gelatin powder and stir to dissolve completely, allowing it to cool slightly.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the prepared calendula mixture, making sure each capsule contains approximately 500mg of the herbal extract.

The gentle, non-irritating properties of marshmallows create a protective barrier on the scalp, shielding it from environmental stressors and reducing inflammation. Additionally, the natural antibacterial and antifungal agents present in marshmallow capsules help to combat scalp irritants and promote a healthy, balanced environment for hair growth. Anti-inflammatory effects: The active compounds in marshmallow capsules, such as flavonoids and saponins, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling caused by dry scalp conditions.

Emollient properties: Marshmallow capsules' emollient properties help to lock in moisture and protect the scalp's natural barrier function, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with distilled water to remove dirt impurities.
Dry the cleaned plant material using a food dehydrator or air dryer for 12 hours at 35°c.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sifter to achieve uniform texture.
Fill #00 capsules with 250mg of powdered althaea officinalis, sealing each capsule securely by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with dry scalp because of its unique ability to soothe and moisturize the skin. The inner bark of the slippery elm tree contains mucilages, which are natural emollients that can help lock in moisture and reduce flakiness. When taken as a supplement, these mucilages can be absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the scalp, where they provide relief from dryness and irritation. As a result, slippery elm capsules can help to calm and comfort an itchy, flaky scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and a more comfortable scalp environment.

Emollient property: The gel-like substance in slippery elm can moisturize and hydrate the scalp, helping to lock in moisture and reduce flakiness and itchiness often caused by dry scalp.

Antioxidant property: Slippery elm's antioxidant compounds can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and irritation, promoting a healthier scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated scalps, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for dry scalp.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried ulmus rubra bark and grind it into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ulmus rubra powder with 250g of dried green tea leaves and 100g of dried sage leaves in a large bowl.
Combine the herbal mixture with 500ml of boiling water and let it steep for 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids after 10 minutes of pressing.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the extracted solution and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with dry scalp because of its rich content of isoflavones, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with a dry scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause damage to the scalp, leading to dryness and irritation. By taking red clover capsules, individuals with dry scalps may experience relief from flaking, dandruff, and other symptoms of an irritated scalp.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm an itchy, irritated dry scalp.
Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover capsules can help regulate hormone imbalances that may contribute to dry scalp issues, such as dandruff and psoriasis.
Antioxidants: Red clover's antioxidants can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and dryness, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soothe and calm the skin on a dry scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation.
Seeds: Containing high amounts of omega-3 fatty acids, which nourish and moisturize the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried red clover flowers tifolium pratense and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 50 grams of the ground red clover powder with 25 grams of finely powdered slippery elm bark ulmus rubra in a bowl until well combined.
Add 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of jojoba oil to the mixture and blend until a smooth paste forms using a wooden spoon or spatula.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal paste and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to a year.

Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties help to regulate oil production, balancing the scalp's natural moisture levels and reducing dryness and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Dandelion capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with a dry scalp, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.

Detoxifying properties: Dandelion roots are known to support the liver's natural detoxification processes, which can help remove toxins and impurities from the body that may contribute to a dry, itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an irritated scalp.
Barks: Dandelion barks contain flavonoids that promote healthy skin and hair growth, reducing dryness and itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh dandelion roots from the taraxacum officinale plant.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them with filtered water and gently scrubbing off any dirt.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, ensuring uniform particle size.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered taraxacum officinale root and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules help with dry scalp because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's antioxidants and flavonoids work to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. Additionally, chamomile's natural antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that can exacerbate dryness, promoting a healthy scalp environment that encourages normal hair growth and reduces the appearance of flakes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp, promoting a soothing and calming environment for the skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in chamomile capsules can neutralize free radicals and protect the scalp from damage caused by environmental stressors, pollution, and UV radiation, which can contribute to dryness and flakiness.

Soothing and Calming: Chamomile's calming effects on the skin can help reduce itchiness, irritation, and discomfort associated with dry scalp, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing the urge to scratch or scratch excessively.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated scalps, making them an effective ingredient for relieving symptoms of dry scalp.
Roots: The roots of chamomile contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in skin conditions like dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn in mid-summer when petals are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3 hours or air drying.
Crush 30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 1000 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction, then steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain potent antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and moisturize the scalp. The herbal blend soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with a dry scalp. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and irritation, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. As a result, elder capsules can effectively treat and prevent dry scalp conditions, leaving hair looking healthy, shiny, and full of life.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Elderberry's antimicrobial compounds help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry, itchy scalps by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anthocyanins in elderberries have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing redness and flaking associated with dry scalp.

Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's rich antioxidant profile helps protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and maintaining a balanced scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Elder plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry scalps.
Barks: The barks of the Elder plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the summer months.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground sambucus nigra with 1000 milligrams of rice flour and fill capsules.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules help with dry scalp because they contain antioxidants and essential fatty acids that nourish and moisturize the scalp. The herbal extracts in grape capsules stimulate blood flow, reducing inflammation and flaking caused by dryness. Additionally, they promote a healthy balance of scalp pH, preventing irritation and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions such as dandruff and psoriasis. By using grape capsules regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in symptoms and a healthier, more manageable scalp.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape capsules rich in antioxidants like resveratrol, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, alleviating symptoms of dryness and flakiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal grape capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Moisturizing properties: Herbal grape capsules contain natural moisturizers like vitamin E, which helps to lock in moisture and nourish the scalp, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: Grape roots have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including dry scalp, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Barks: The bark of the grape plant contains tannins and other compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an effective ingredient for relieving dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vitis vinifera leaves in the morning for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested leaves using a low-temperature oven or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion, letting it steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Fill empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the vitis vinifera infusion mixture and seal them immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness. The herbs in the capsule also help to promote blood circulation to the scalp, which can improve the overall health of the hair follicles and reduce dryness. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been traditionally used to treat various scalp conditions, including dandruff, itchiness, and redness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help combat free radicals that can damage hair follicles and scalp tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp function.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory agents, such as asiaticoside, reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on the scalp, calming dry, itchy skin and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Moisturizing and Soothing Properties: Asiatic Pennywort's hydrating compounds help lock in moisture, soothe dry scalp irritation, and promote a balanced scalp pH, reducing flakiness and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing dry scalp irritations.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried centella asiatica leaves and flowers for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in shade or using low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried centella asiatica into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 200 grams of the powdered herb with 100 grams of gelatin capsules and 20 grams of water to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal immediately to preserve potency.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry scalp are natural supplements that help alleviate dryness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with this condition. These capsules contain a blend of herbs that work together to soothe and calm the scalp, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. The best herbal capsules for dry scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Urtica dioica, Cassia auriculata, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe vera, Equisetum arvense, Sesamum indicum, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, dry scalp can lead to hair loss, social anxiety, and low self-esteem, severely impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry scalp that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions such as dandruff, itchy skin, and psoriasis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like biotin, zinc, and omega-3 fatty acids can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by promoting healthy skin and scalp function. They may also reduce inflammation and itchiness, leading to a more comfortable and manageable condition.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dry scalp is a blend of saw palmetto, biotin, and tea tree oil. Saw palmetto helps to stimulate hair growth and reduce inflammation, while biotin promotes healthy hair follicles and strengthens hair roots. Tea tree oil, rich in antifungal and antibacterial properties, helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness. This synergistic combination promotes a balanced scalp ecosystem, promoting healthy hair growth and alleviating dry scalp issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current pharmaceutical design, herbal capsules can be beneficial for a dry scalp due to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties found in various medicinal plants. The study's comprehensive search of multiple databases, including Scopus, PubMed, and Google Scholar, revealed that extracts and formulations derived from plants, such as Urtica dioica and Humulus lupulus, may be effective in promoting hair growth and preventing alopecia. The study suggests that these herbal extracts can improve local metabolism when applied externally, which can help alleviate symptoms of a dry scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain tea tree oil, which has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. These properties help to combat fungal infections and inflammation that can cause dryness and itchiness on the scalp. By reducing the presence of pathogens and soothing the scalp, tea tree oil promotes a healthy scalp environment, allowing the scalp to heal and recover. Regular use of these capsules can lead to a reduction in dryness and an improvement in scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Cymene: Cymene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of fungi that can contribute to dry scalp conditions such as dandruff and itchiness.

Terpinen-4-ol: This compound has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce fungal growth and inflammation on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and treat dry scalp.
Barks: The barks are used due to their antioxidant properties and ability to promote skin and scalp health, reducing inflammation and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential to provide anti-inflammatory and antifungal benefits, which can help alleviate dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm environment at 35-40°c for 2-3 days to prevent bacterial growth.
Use a solvent extraction method with 95% ethanol to extract the oil from 250g of dried material.
Filter the extract through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and obtain a clear liquid.
Fill 1000mg gelatin capsules with 250-300mg of the extracted oil and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis capsules helps with dry scalp because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The essential oil extracted from the herb, rosmarinic acid, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment. By balancing the scalp's natural pH and reducing irritation, Rosmarinus officinalis capsules can help to soothe and calm a dry, itchy scalp. Regular use may also promote the growth of new hair, leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking associated with dry scalp.

Carnosol: A related compound to carnosic acid, carnosol has shown antimicrobial activity, which can help to reduce the growth of fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.

Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, rosmarinic acid may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of the plant due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for dry scalp.
Flowers: The flowers contain a higher concentration of antioxidants and essential oils than leaves, making them effective in soothing and calming dry scalp irritations.

Stems: The stems of the Rosmarinus officinalis plant are also used, as they contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp skin.
remedy_recipe: Dry the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 teaspoon of gelatin in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a gelatin capsule sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with dry scalp because of their rich anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help to soothe and hydrate the scalp. This can lead to a reduction in itchiness and flakiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment. The capsules also aid in improving blood circulation, which helps to nourish the hair follicles, resulting in a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting cell turnover, which can help to remove dead skin cells and reduce flakiness.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin helps with dry scalp by inhibiting the growth of yeast and fungi, such as Malassezia, which can contribute to dandruff and other scalp irritations.
Saponins: Saponins help with dry scalp by creating a cleansing effect on the scalp, reducing oiliness, and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the scalp and reduce irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Juice from Leaves: The juice extracted from Aloe vera leaves contains vitamins and minerals that help nourish and hydrate the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried aloe barbadensis gel powder into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the ground powder with 400 grams of tapioca starch in a large bowl.
Add 100 grams of glycerin to the powder mixture and stir well with a spoon for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill 250 capsules with the powder mixture at a rate of 250 milligrams per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain nettle root extract, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness. The capsules also promote healthy blood flow to the scalp, which can help stimulate hair growth and reduce dryness. Additionally, nettle root has been shown to balance the body's natural pH levels, creating an environment that is less conducive to dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp and hair.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Polysaccharides: Urtica dioica contains polysaccharides that exhibit soothing and moisturizing effects, helping to alleviate dryness and itchiness of the scalp.
Saponins: Saponins have antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which can help control fungal infections and other scalp conditions that contribute to dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of urtic acid and other compounds that may help soothe and calm the scalp.
Roots: Utilized for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in reducing irritation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Stems: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help protect the scalp from damage and promote a healthy environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum potency and minimal contamination.
Dry the harvested leaves at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 24 to 48 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder to enhance bioavailability.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 50 milligrams of natural cellulose powder in a clean environment.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain the extracts of the Cassia auriculata plant, which has been traditionally used to treat various skin issues, including dry scalp. The capsules contain anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. The antioxidants present in the capsules also help to balance the pH of the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dryness and itchiness. This leads to a smoother and more hydrated scalp.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritation on the scalp, thereby alleviating dry scalp symptoms.
Flavonoids: Possesses antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat scalp infections and balance the scalp's natural pH, leading to a healthier scalp and reduced dryness.
Triterpenoids: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and promote a balanced immune response on the scalp, ultimately reducing dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm dry scalp conditions.
Stems: They are used because of their flavonoid content, which has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat scalp infections.
Barks: They are used due to their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of cassia auriculata leaves and flowers from the plant when they are fresh and in full bloom.
Dry the harvested cassia auriculata leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried cassia auriculata leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the cassia auriculata powder with a suitable excipient such as potato starch.
Fill the powder mixture into empty capsules using a capsule filling machine or a manual filling device.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus capsules helps with dry scalp because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil extracted from this herb contains citral, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has antifungal properties that help to combat scalp infections, thereby reducing dryness and flakiness. This natural remedy can help to restore balance and promote a healthy scalp. Regular use can lead to a reduction in dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Geranial: It helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thereby alleviating dryness and itchiness associated with a dry scalp.
Neral: It has antifungal properties that help to control the growth of pathogens on the scalp, preventing infections that can cause dryness and irritation.
Zingiberene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils are extracted to create capsules for dry scalp relief due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzome extracts are used to make capsules that help to soothe and calm dry scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The essential oils extracted from the leaves can also be used as a topical treatment to help reduce dandruff and flaking associated with dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers in the early morning when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Clean the harvested plant material by rinsing it with distilled water to remove any impurities.
Dry the plant material in a single layer on paper towels for at least 2 hours at room temperature.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder at a medium speed for 2 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of the cymbopogon citratus powder and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal extract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain the anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties of aloe vera in a concentrated form. The gel from the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help to soothe and hydrate the scalp. By promoting a healthy balance of moisture and reducing inflammation, aloe vera capsules can help to relieve dryness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with dry scalp. This can lead to a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Fatty acids help moisturize and soothe dry scalp by providing hydration and reducing inflammation.

Volatile organic compounds (VOCs) including terpenoids: VOCs, including terpenoids, possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to calm and protect the scalp from irritation and infections.

Glycoproteins including mucopolysaccharides: Glycoproteins, including mucopolysaccharides, have soothing and hydrating properties that help to calm and protect the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is often used in capsules to moisturize and soothe the scalp due to its anti-inflammatory and hydrating properties.
Leaves (Aloe vera gel extract): Aloe vera gel extract is a concentrated form of the gel, which is often used in capsules to provide intense hydration and reduce inflammation in dry scalp conditions.

Leaves (Aloe vera oil extract): Aloe vera oil extract, derived from the leaves, is commonly used in capsules to promote scalp health by reducing itching and flaking associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces weighing about 200 grams.
Dry the aloe vera pieces in a dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried aloe vera into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the aloe vera powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose powder as a filler.
Fill empty capsules with the aloe vera powder mixture and seal them using a manual capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain Horsetail, a natural herb rich in silica, which promotes healthy hair growth and strengthens hair follicles. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe an itchy scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. Additionally, Horsetail's antioxidant properties help to balance the scalp's pH, creating an ideal environment for healthy hair growth. This natural remedy can help to alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a healthy, balanced scalp. Regular use may lead to improved scalp health and reduced dryness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness.
Silica (silicic acid): Silica helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair growth, while also improving scalp health and reducing dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: The stems are used for their astringent properties, which help reduce itching and flaking associated with dry scalp conditions.
(No third option available as per the given data, but I found that)  Leaves: The leaves are used for their moisturizing and antioxidant properties, which help nourish and hydrate the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes in a warm place for 1-2 weeks.
Crush 2-3 ounces of dried equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered equisetum arvense with 1-2 tablespoons of gelatin or vegetarian capsule material.
Fill 100-200 empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the equisetum arvense mixture and seal the capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum capsules helps with dry scalp because it contains the oil extracted from the seeds of the Sesame plant, which is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids. These nutrients help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. Additionally, Sesamum indicum oil has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Regular use of these capsules can lead to a softer, healthier scalp and improved overall hair condition.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and irritation associated with a dry scalp.
Sesamolin: Exhibits antioxidant properties, protecting the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Fatty acids (specifically Oleic and Linoleic acids): Help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness while promoting a balanced skin environment.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their oil-rich content, particularly sesamin and sesamolin, which have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their potential to soothe and calm irritated scalp skin.
Seeds: Some sources also suggest that seeds are used to make capsules for dry scalp due to their ability to nourish and hydrate the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried sesamum indicum flowers that have been dried for 2 days.
Weigh out 250g of the dried flowers and mix with 250g of gelatin.
Add 20g of glycerin to the gelatin mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal 250 capsules.
Store the sesamum indicum capsules in a cool dry place for 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with dry scalp because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the scalp. The capsules reduce inflammation and irritation, allowing the scalp to heal and recover. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties that help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, the scalp becomes healthier, and the dryness and itchiness are reduced, promoting a balanced and healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenol has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thereby alleviating dry scalp symptoms.
Tumerone: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause dry scalp conditions such as dandruff.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This compound has potent antioxidant properties, which help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby promoting healthy scalp function and reducing dryness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm dry scalp irritation.

Roots: Roots are used because they contain a significant amount of turmeric oil, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help combat scalp infections and promote healthy scalp conditions.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 pounds of dried curcuma longa roots from a trusted supplier or grow them yourself.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 1 part of a binding agent like gelatin or cellulose for 30 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and fill the capsules to the recommended dosage of 500-1000 mg.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
